Capital City College Group (CCCG) today November) announced that merging with College Haringey, Enfield North East London (CONEL), making Group largest further education training provider London.
With CONEL joining, Capital City College Group will provide learning 37,000 students aged over, sites across London South East, well providing high-quality training service employers.
part overriding commitment provide next generation skilled Londoners, enlarged Group nationally recognised specialisms areas crucial UK's future, such construction, digital technology, healthcare, hospitality, rail engineering science, last year, CCCG's colleges trained more than 2,000 apprentices behalf 1,000 employers.
three colleges (Westminster Kingsway College, City Islington College, College Haringey, Enfield North East London), which currently have 'Good' OfSTED grades, will maintain their individual operating brands.
And, with apprenticeships forming increasingly important part further education provision, three colleges' services will supported Capital City College Training (CCCT), launched CCCG earlier this year focus meeting employers' training needs provide widest range apprenticeships London.
Andy Forbes, Executive Principal CONEL, said: "The merger CONEL into CCCG will create three strong, confident colleges help each grow, develop thrive.
"CONEL fantastic college, extremely proud that time joining CCCG that we're very much game, having recently been named Choices Survey best college England student satisfaction London employer satisfaction second year running.
"Our commitment deliver high quality vocational training higher education courses Haringey Enfield remains unchanged continue focus success part this partnership.
Andy Wilson, CCCG, said: very excited that CONEL joining group bringing together three London's most successful colleges create education training powerhouse regional college London, which will deliver exceptional benefits local communities businesses.

dollar remained shaky footing start week markets awaited this week's long-anticipated Federal Reserve meeting, when decision will announced whether will raise rates this year first time nearly years.
Last week greenback largely mixed pulled back significantly against several major currencies, most notably Japanese yen, while strengthening against commodity currencies like Canadian dollar Australian dollar.
dollar pullback initially prompted prior week sharp surge euro European Central Bank took measures stimulate European economy.
This exacerbated fact that much broad-based expectations rate hike this week already been substantially priced into strong dollar.
USD/JPY, retreat been especially severe.
this currency pair, dollar pullback combined with volatility global equity markets that prompted yen-buying, pushing USD/JPY down more than one-month below 121.00.
euro pound also gained against falling dollar, with EUR/USD briefly hitting high above 1.1000 GBP/USD temporarily reaching above 1.5200.
case commodities commodity currencies, gold crude both fell substantially, with taking especially severe slide ever-growing oversupply fears.
This placed tremendous pressure Canadian dollar, with USD/CAD reaching well above 1.3600 target.
Australian dollar also took commodity-linked fell back down support around 0.7200 level.
This past Friday brought economic data events that were failed provide support weakening dollar.
Core Retail Sales rose November more than expected, Producer Price Index, indicator inflation, also more than expected.
Despite these positive signs pointing even better case rate hike this week, dollar continued fall Friday against most other major currencies, with continued exception commodity currencies, stock markets also felt increasingly severe pressure.
this Wednesday's meeting draws ever closer, some have deemed rate hike almost foregone conclusion, dollar likely find some support, especially Fed's statement includes some hawkish language reference ongoing monetary tightening cycle.
Even there such language, however, monetary policy divergence between most other major central banks currently well-defined should contribute continued dollar strength going into 2016.

TODAY NETWORK will host first ever livestream American Football Coaches Association Awards Show (AFCA) green carpet from Charlotte, N.C.
TODAY NETWORK will host first ever livestream American Football Coaches Association Awards Show (AFCA) green carpet tonight from Charlotte, N.C. After Alabama's thrilling comeback victory over Georgia national title game last night, AFCA Awards Show will bring together 8,000 coaches former coaches celebrate best college football this season.
event will live TODAY.com TODAY Sports' Facebook page.
awards show, presented Amway, will hosted Rick Neuheisel, former UCLA coach Rose Bowl-winning quarterback Bruins, well former ESPN broadcaster Bonnie Bernstein.
night's honors will include Coach Year, Allstate AFCA Good Works Team® Honorary Head Coach, TODAY Sports Coach's Play Call Year.
Football fans look forward appearances Central Florida's (and Nebraska's) Scott Frost, former Huskers coach Frank Solich, Georgia's Kirby Smart, Virginia's Bronco Mendenhall, Wisconsin's Paul Chryst, Northwestern's Fitzgerald, Iowa State's Matt Campbell, Fresno State's Jeff Tedford, Liberty's Turner Gill.
Former Marshall coach Jack Lengyel will honored, along with Jenks (Okla.) High School coach Allan Trimble, will receive Power Influence Award fights ALS.
Coaches from FCS, Division Division NAIA will also recognized.
Before show's kickoff, there will green-carpet event hosted TODAY Sports Pulse's Trysta Krick Adam Woodard, along with TODAY Sports national college football writer George Schroeder.
will feature interviews with coaches they arrive green carpet will debut original videos shot this year's convention TODAY Sports.

green-carpet event will livestreamed Facebook beginning p.m.
from NASCAR Hall Fame, awards show will live starting p.m.

It's hard miss signs posted around campus notifying students asbestos abatement projects.
Most recently, they have been displayed doors Library Café, Ingersoll Hall, Whitehead Hall, Boylan Hall, Whitman Theatre, causing many students worried about health consequences these abatement projects after all, everyone seen those mesothelioma advertisements listing dangers asbestos over ominous music.
According Francis Fitzgerald, Brooklyn College's assistant vice president facilities planning operations, there asbestos abatement projects currently happening.
guidelines Office Environmental Health Safety, these notices must placed entire duration construction project, which actual asbestos removal might only small portion.
amount time requires remove asbestos depends complex project range from several weeks.
Asbestos generic name group minerals that resist heat corrosion very well, therefore used very popular construction projects.
However, these minerals turned serious health hazards; they have tiny fibers that, when directly breathed cause scar tissue lungs.
This scarring leads decreased lung function even cause lung cancer.
When this cancer affects mesothelial tissue, which lines several your organs, called mesothelioma.
1989, Environmental Protection Agency issued asbestos use.
However, because asbestos only dangerous when directly inhaled, asbestos that already safely contained construction generally removed.

when buildings campus undergo construction repair that would disturb these contained minerals, third-party firms contracted remove asbestos measure around these construction projects ensure that there fibers.
office's website lists precautions that taken when these projects worked example, contractors "negative air" machine that suctions through special filters that remove asbestos particles.
result these measures, they declare that safe building while abatement happening.
